title: sideroxylon borbonicum var. borbonicum
intro: Sideroxylon borbonicum var. borbonicum, also known as the Bourbon Island Black Olive or Bois d'Inde, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and providing relief from fever and digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Sideroxylon borbonicum var. borbonicum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits.
